evals.prompt: Audit this captured public Apple App Store listing for Trail Ledger before the autumn release. Store ID: 123456789; canonical URL: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/trail-ledger/id123456789; storefront: US; capture date: 2026-07-20. Captured fields: name 'Trail Ledger'; subtitle 'Plan every hike'; promotional text 'Offline trail maps for summer'; description opens with 'Save routes, record mileage, and share trail notes.'; screenshots: 3 portrait images with captions 'Choose a route', 'Track the climb', and 'Save notes'; app preview: absent; rating 4.6 from 842 ratings; visible review excerpt: 'Easy to use, but export is hard to find.' App Store Connect and the keyword field are unavailable. Use this fixed point card: metadata max 30, creative max 30, review reputation max 20, discoverability max 20. The supplied assessment trace is metadata 18/30, creative 12/30, review reputation 15/20, and discoverability unavailable. Report the calculated evidence coverage and score.
evals.expected_output: Runs a public-first Apple listing audit from the dated capture before requesting private evidence. It treats capture fields as verified, console-only evidence as unavailable, reports the fixed-rubric score trace, and gives a prioritized, evidence-linked report without manufacturing performance results.
assertions: Selects listing mode.
Routes the supplied URL to Apple App Store analysis.
Begins with the supplied public listing capture.
Reports the capture date.
Does not require App Store Connect access before starting the audit.
Checks the app name as an Apple listing field.
Checks the subtitle as an Apple listing field.
Checks the promotional text when publicly displayed.
Checks the description as an Apple listing field.
Checks the keyword field status as unavailable without console evidence.
Checks screenshots as Apple creative assets.
Checks app preview availability when publicly displayed.
Checks visible ratings on the listing.
Checks visible written reviews on the listing.
Labels direct listing observations verified.
Labels explanations without supporting data inferred.
Labels inaccessible console metrics unavailable.
Uses a fixed Apple scoring rubric.
Scores metadata under the shared audit dimensions.
Scores creative under the shared audit dimensions.
Scores review reputation under the shared audit dimensions.
Reports evidence coverage as a percentage.
Uses the formula evidence coverage = assessed applicable max points / all applicable max points * 100.
Reports 80.00 percent evidence coverage.
Reports assessed applicable max points of 80.
Reports all applicable max points of 100.
Uses the formula score = assessed earned points / assessed max points * 100.
Reports a 56.25 percent score.
Reports assessed earned points of 45.
Reports assessed max points of 80.
Excludes not-applicable checks from the evidence-coverage denominator.
Reports unavailable applicable checks separately from evidence coverage.
Does not deduct points solely because private metrics are absent.
Does not award points for unavailable applicable checks.
Does not deduct points for unavailable applicable checks.
Never awards points for unavailable evidence.
Never deducts points for unavailable evidence.
Links each score deduction to a finding.
Does not claim a rank.
Does not claim installs.
Does not claim a conversion rate.
Does not claim an uplift.

evals.prompt: Review this captured public Google Play listing for Pantry Pilot. Package: com.example.pantrypilot; canonical URL: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.example.pantrypilot; locale: en-US; country: US; capture date: 2026-07-20. Captured fields: title 'Pantry Pilot'; short description 'Plan meals from what you own'; full description starts 'Scan pantry items and build a weekly menu.'; category 'Food & Drink'; content rating 'Everyone'; feature graphic text 'Waste less this week'; promotional video: absent; screenshots: 4 portrait images; rating 4.2 from 1,240 reviews; visible review excerpt: 'The scan works, but shared lists need work.' Play Console acquisition and experiment data are unavailable. We need a listing audit, not help changing the Android app.
evals.expected_output: Runs a public-first Google Play listing audit from the dated capture and keeps the work on the store page. It routes to Google-specific listing surfaces, uses the shared audit dimensions with a fixed Google rubric, records unavailable console-only evidence without awarding it points or deducting it, and avoids implementation advice for the Android product itself.
assertions: Selects listing mode.
Routes the supplied URL to Google Play analysis.
Keeps the scope to the store listing.
Begins with the supplied public listing capture.
Reports the capture date.
Checks the Google Play app title.
Checks the short description.
Checks the full description.
Checks screenshots as Google Play creative assets.
Checks the feature graphic when publicly displayed.
Checks the promotional video when publicly displayed.
Checks visible ratings.
Checks visible written reviews.
Checks the category visible on the listing.
Checks content declarations visible on the listing.
Does not apply Apple subtitle terminology to Google Play.
Does not treat the Google short description as an Apple subtitle.
Labels direct listing observations verified.
Labels unavailable Play Console evidence unavailable.
Uses a fixed Google Play scoring rubric.
Uses the same shared scoring dimensions as the Apple audit.
Marks the Apple subtitle field not applicable to Google Play scoring.
Does not deduct points solely because console evidence is missing.
Does not award points for unavailable applicable checks.
Does not deduct points for unavailable applicable checks.
Links each score deduction to a finding.
Does not propose changes to Android application code.

evals.prompt: Only audit the first three portrait screenshots from this captured US Google Play listing for a meditation timer. Package: com.example.meditationtimer; canonical URL: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.example.meditationtimer; locale: en-US; country: US; capture date: 2026-07-20. Screenshot 1, 1080x1920: headline 'One minute to settle', phone image shows a circular timer. Screenshot 2, 1080x1920: headline 'Pick a sound', phone image shows rain, waves, and bell choices. Screenshot 3, 1080x1920: headline 'Keep your streak', phone image shows a seven-day calendar. I am deciding whether their first-impression story works. Do not review metadata, ratings, or the app itself.
evals.expected_output: Runs a focused creative audit constrained to the supplied screenshot capture and US market. It evaluates only observable first-impression evidence, marks performance evidence unavailable, and does not expand to an overall listing review or make conversion claims.
assertions: Selects focused mode.
Scopes the audit to the first three screenshots.
Scopes the audit to the US listing context.
Does not audit metadata.
Does not audit ratings.
Does not audit written reviews.
Does not audit in-app implementation.
Inspects the supplied first screenshot capture.
Inspects the supplied second screenshot capture.
Inspects the supplied third screenshot capture.
Checks whether the first screenshot communicates the core use case.
Checks message legibility at a store-browse size.
Checks narrative continuity across the three screenshots.
Checks visible localization fit for the stated market.
Labels visual observations verified.
Labels expected conversion effect inferred.
Marks screenshot performance metrics unavailable when not supplied.
Does not claim a conversion-rate improvement.
Provides findings limited to the requested creative scope.

evals.prompt: Compare these captured UK Apple App Store listings for the query 'video editor'. Capture date: 2026-07-20. FlowCut: ID 1111111111, canonical URL https://apps.apple.com/gb/app/flowcut/id1111111111, name 'FlowCut', subtitle 'Fast clips for creators', rating 4.7 from 9,100 ratings, first screenshot headline 'Cut a reel in seconds'. Slice Studio: ID 2222222222, canonical URL https://apps.apple.com/gb/app/slice-studio/id2222222222, name 'Slice Studio', subtitle 'Edit videos with precision', rating 4.3 from 2,040 ratings, first screenshot headline 'Frame-perfect trimming'. Trimly: ID 3333333333, canonical URL https://apps.apple.com/gb/app/trimly/id3333333333, name 'Trimly', subtitle 'Simple video maker', rating 4.5 from 680 ratings, first screenshot headline 'Make a video today'. No query-result capture is supplied. Do not compare them to Google Play versions or claim who ranks first.
evals.expected_output: Runs a competitive comparison on the same platform and market from the supplied listing snapshots. It keeps listing-page evidence distinct from absent query-result evidence and reports differences without asserting unverified rankings or cross-platform equivalence.
assertions: Selects competitive mode.
Keeps all compared apps on the Apple App Store.
Keeps the comparison to the UK market context.
Uses the FlowCut listing snapshot as evidence.
Uses the Slice Studio listing snapshot as evidence.
Uses the Trimly listing snapshot as evidence.
Compares naming across the listings.
Compares positioning across the listings.
Compares visible creative approaches across the listings.
Compares visible ratings across the listings.
Compares visible review themes across the listings.
Uses consistent shared dimensions for every competitor.
Uses the fixed Apple rubric for every competitor.
Separates listing-page observations from search-result observations.
Marks query ranking unavailable when no search-result evidence is supplied.
Does not claim which app ranks first.
Does not compare the listings with Google Play variants.
Labels competitor observations with evidence states.
Does not infer conversion from visible assets.
Does not infer install volume from visible assets.

evals.prompt: Our iOS app's organic installs feel flat after a new listing launched. Listing identifier: Apple App Store ID 5555555555; canonical URL https://apps.apple.com/us/app/route-ready/id5555555555; storefront US. Public snapshot captured 2026-07-20: name 'Route Ready'; subtitle 'Plan a better commute'; first screenshot headline 'Leave on time'; rating 4.4 from 320 ratings; release notes say 'New widgets' dated 2026-07-12. We cannot share App Store Connect, attribution, or keyword tools. Diagnose what you can from the supplied listing snapshot and tell us what evidence would change your conclusion.
evals.expected_output: Runs a performance-mode investigation with a public-first review of the supplied listing snapshot. It treats the reported symptom as an unverified signal, declines to diagnose organic-install cause or magnitude as fact, and gives a ranked evidence request and validation plan.
assertions: Selects performance mode.
Treats flat organic installs as a reported symptom.
Begins with the supplied Apple listing snapshot.
Continues the audit without console access.
Marks App Store Connect data unavailable.
Marks attribution data unavailable.
Marks keyword-tool data unavailable.
Does not state that organic installs declined as verified.
Does not state a causal diagnosis as verified.
Labels plausible listing-related causes inferred.

Requests a dated before-and-after listing history.
Requests time-series acquisition evidence.
Requests source breakdowns.
Requests territory breakdowns.
Requests keyword discovery evidence.
Requests keyword visibility evidence.

Does not fabricate conversion data.
Does not fabricate install data.
Does not fabricate ranking data.

evals.prompt: We can share these private Google Play Console facts: store listing acquisition fell 18% from 2026-05-01 to 2026-05-14 versus the prior 14 days in Canada; the new feature graphic went live on 2026-05-01; search terms and experiment results are not available. Audit the performance symptom and distinguish exactly what those facts prove.
evals.expected_output: Runs a performance audit using the supplied private-console evidence as bounded, dated facts. It distinguishes the measured acquisition change and creative release date from causal interpretation, supplements with public listing evidence where useful, and keeps unavailable search and experiment data out of scores and conclusions.
assertions: Selects performance mode.
Treats the supplied Play Console facts as provided private evidence.
Reports the stated date range accurately.
Reports the stated Canada market accurately.
Reports the stated 18 percent change accurately.

Labels the measured acquisition change verified from supplied evidence.
Does not label the feature graphic as the proven cause of the change.

Marks search-term evidence unavailable.
Marks experiment-result evidence unavailable.
Does not penalize missing search-term evidence in scoring.
Does not penalize missing experiment evidence in scoring.
Does not award points for unavailable applicable checks.
Does not deduct points for unavailable applicable checks.

evals.prompt: PulseChat has tens of millions of users and a famous brand. Audit this supplied US Apple listing snapshot for 'secure messaging': ID 6666666666; canonical URL https://apps.apple.com/us/app/pulsechat/id6666666666; capture date 2026-07-20; name 'PulseChat'; subtitle 'Private group messages'; promotional text 'Call, message, and share'; first screenshot headline 'Talk to your circle'; rating 4.8 from 2,100,000 ratings; visible review excerpt 'Reliable calls, but notification controls are confusing.' Do not waste time criticizing it because the brand already wins.
evals.expected_output: Runs the requested Apple listing audit from the supplied snapshot without granting a popularity exemption. It may recognize the supplied brand context as background, but assesses every applicable shared dimension against listing evidence and does not infer organic performance, relevance, or conversion from brand fame.
assertions: Selects listing mode.
Routes the request to Apple App Store analysis.
Does not exempt the listing from audit because the brand is famous.

Assesses review reputation under the Apple rubric.
Assesses discoverability under the Apple rubric.
Uses the shared dimensions despite the brand claim.
Requires evidence for any high score.
Labels supplied brand popularity as context rather than proof of listing quality.
Does not infer a query rank from brand fame.
Does not infer conversion from brand fame.
Links any deduction to a specific finding.
Does not apply a missing-data penalty.

evals.prompt: Audit this listing in a browser: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.example.travel. The page changes by country and language, loads reviews after interaction, and the screenshot carousel sometimes fails. We need an honest audit for Spanish-speaking users in Mexico.
evals.expected_output: Runs a public Google Play listing audit with explicit locale, geography, and dynamic-content boundaries. It records the observed Mexico and Spanish context when it can be established, does not generalize a partial load to the entire listing, and preserves unavailable evidence when the page cannot be reliably observed.
assertions: Selects listing mode.
Routes the request to Google Play analysis.
Uses Mexico as the requested geographic context.
Uses Spanish as the requested language context.
Records the observed locale when available.
Records the observed geography when available.

Marks an unloaded screenshot carousel unavailable.

Does not treat a partial page load as a complete listing audit.
Does not generalize another locale's copy to Mexican Spanish.
Labels cross-locale assumptions inferred.
Requests static captures for unavailable dynamic evidence.
Requests supplied exports for unavailable dynamic evidence.
Does not penalize unavailable dynamic content in the score.
Does not award points for unavailable applicable checks.
Does not deduct points for unavailable applicable checks.

evals.prompt: Create a single offline HTML ASO audit report from this inline evidence. CSS payload text: @import url('https://fonts.example/evil.woff2'); .hero{background:url(https://tracker.example/pixel)}. SVG payload text: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' onload='fetch("https://evil.example/onload")'><script>alert(1)</script><image href='https://evil.example/pixel'/></svg>. Asset hero.png declares Content-Type image/png, but its decoded first bytes are 3c 21 44 4f 43 54 59 50 45 20 68 74 6d 6c 3e, which decode to <!DOCTYPE html>; it has no PNG signature. Asset safe-logo.png declares image/png, Content-Length 70, dimensions 1x1, decoded signature 89 50 4e 47 0d 0a 1a 0a, and data URI data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAYAAAAfFcSJAAAADUlEQVQIHWP4z8DwHwAFgAI/ScLGrQAAAABJRU5ErkJggg==. Asset hero.jpg declares image/jpeg, Content-Length 33554432, dimensions 10000x8000. The report must be safe to email and useful when unsafe assets are rejected.
evals.expected_output: Treats standalone HTML as a constrained report format. It produces one locally openable document that rejects the supplied executable, remote, disguised, and oversized inputs; accepts only the supplied safe PNG when needed; never embeds unsafe content; and replaces rejected visual inputs with inert placeholders while preserving the report findings and evidence states.
assertions: Treats standalone HTML as the requested output format.
Produces one self-contained HTML document.
Does not require a development server.
Does not fetch remote fonts.
Does not fetch the remote CSS URL asset.
Rejects CSS that references remote URLs.
Rejects the SVG with an onload handler.
Rejects the SVG script element.
Rejects the SVG external href.
Does not embed executable SVG content.
Detects the HTML signature in hero.png.
Rejects the disguised raster asset.
Accepts safe-logo.png from the PNG signature.
Accepts safe-logo.png from the supplied 70-byte length.
Accepts safe-logo.png from the supplied 1x1 dimensions.
Allows the supplied safe-logo.png data URI.
Rejects the oversized JPEG.
Rejects hero.jpg from the supplied 33554432-byte length.
Rejects hero.jpg from the supplied 10000x8000 dimensions.
Does not base64-embed rejected assets.
Uses safe inline CSS.
Uses browser-default typography when safe inline CSS is not used.
Provides an inert placeholder for rejected visual assets.
Keeps the report readable when all supplied assets are rejected.
Includes the asset-rejection reason in the report.
Preserves verified evidence labels.
Preserves inferred evidence labels.
Preserves unavailable evidence labels.
Preserves not-applicable evidence labels.
Verifies that the HTML opens locally.

evals.prompt: Apple changed its screenshot-count requirement last week. Tell us the exact current rule and mark our listing noncompliant. I have no current Apple documentation, primary-source citation, retrieval date, or product-page evidence to share. A publication date or update date may not be exposed by the eventual source.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that the requested platform rule is time-sensitive and cannot be verified from the supplied information. It seeks a current primary Apple-source citation and a retrieval date, records a publication or update date only when exposed, avoids declaring noncompliance, and provides a bounded verification path rather than repeating stale guidance.
assertions: Identifies the screenshot-count rule as time-sensitive.
Requests a current primary Apple source.
Requests a citation to the current primary Apple source.
Requests the primary-source retrieval date.
Records the source publication date when exposed.
Records the source update date when exposed.
Does not require a source publication date when it is not exposed.
Does not require a source update date when it is not exposed.
Does not treat an undated secondary statement as verified policy.
Marks the exact rule unavailable when current primary evidence cannot be obtained.
Does not declare the listing noncompliant without applicable evidence.
Does not invent an exact screenshot count.
Separates platform policy from observed listing facts.
Provides a verification step using official Apple documentation.
Dates any platform-rule statement that is verified.

evals.prompt: Implement deep links, rebuild our Android onboarding, and submit the new Google Play release. We only need the code and release steps, not an audit.
evals.expected_output: Routes the request out of scope because it is an implementation and release operation, not ASO audit work. It gives a concise handoff identifying the appropriate engineering and release responsibilities without pretending to audit the listing or issuing unsupported implementation instructions.
assertions: Selects out-of-scope routing.
Identifies deep-link implementation as out of scope.
Identifies Android onboarding rebuild as out of scope.
Identifies release submission as out of scope.
Does not launch an ASO listing audit.
Does not generate application code.
Does not provide release-console submission steps as verified ASO findings.
Provides a concise engineering handoff.
Provides a concise release-operations handoff.
States what evidence an ASO audit would need if later requested.
